sections.title: When police may arrest without warrant
sections.summary: Police can arrest a person without a warrant if they commit a cognizable offence in their presence or if a reasonable complaint exists.
sections.example: If someone is seen snatching a chain, the police can arrest them immediately without waiting for a court order.

sections.title: Arrest how made
sections.summary: Explains the procedure for arrest, emphasizing that a woman should only be arrested by a female officer and only during daylight hours.
sections.example: A woman cannot be arrested after sunset or before sunrise except in extraordinary circumstances and with a magistrate's permission.

chapters.title: Provisions as to Bail and Bonds

sections.title: When bail may be taken in case of non-bailable offence
sections.summary: Provides guidelines for when a person accused of a serious (non-bailable) crime can still apply for bail from a court.
sections.example: If someone is accused of a crime but is very old or ill, the court might grant them bail even if the crime is serious.

sections.title: Direction for grant of bail to person apprehending arrest (Anticipatory Bail)
sections.summary: Allows a person to apply for bail in advance if they believe they might be arrested for a non-bailable offence.
sections.example: If you suspect a false case is being filed against you, you can apply to the High Court or Sessions Court for protection from arrest.
